HowItWorksClientsStep1: Sign in with GitHub or Google and set up your payment method
HowItWorksClientsStep2: Create a task with a description, repository link, and bounty amount
HowItWorksClientsStep3: Developers submit public pull requests for your task
HowItWorksClientsStep4: Review all submitted pull requests and approve the best one
HowItWorksClientsStep5: You are automatically charged the bounty and the developer gets paid
HowItWorksDescriptionHeading: How PrTask Works — Complete Guide to Earning and Hiring
HowItWorksDescriptionParagraph1: PrTask streamlines the process of connecting developers with paid software work through a pull request-based workflow that mirrors how professional development teams already operate. The process begins with signing up using your GitHub, Google, Microsoft, or Okta account, which takes seconds and requires no separate registration form. Once authenticated, developers can immediately browse available tasks, while clients can start creating tasks and setting bounties right away. The entire lifecycle from task creation to payment disbursement follows a predictable, transparent workflow with clear steps at every stage.
HowItWorksDescriptionParagraph2: For developers, the workflow is straightforward: browse tasks, find one matching your skills, review the requirements and linked GitHub repository, implement the solution in your own fork, and submit a pull request through PrTask. The task owner reviews your code following standard GitHub review practices, may request changes, and ultimately approves and merges your contribution. Upon merge, payment is triggered automatically — no invoicing, no payment requests, no waiting for manual processing. The bounty is disbursed directly to your configured payout method.
HowItWorksDescriptionParagraph3: For clients, PrTask simplifies the hiring process by eliminating interviews, contracts, and scope negotiations. Create a task with a clear description, set the complexity level and bounty amount, link it to your GitHub repository, and publish it to the marketplace. Developers worldwide can then discover and work on your task. You review submissions as pull requests with full code diff visibility, request changes if needed, and approve when satisfied. The platform handles payment processing, developer verification, and dispute resolution, letting you focus on reviewing code quality rather than managing freelancer relationships.
HowItWorksWorkflowDescription: Follow these steps to complete the full workflow from login to payment.
HowItWorksWorkflowStep10Description: Add inline comments on specific lines or highlighted code that need changes.
HowItWorksWorkflowStep11Description: Developer applies fixes, replies to comments, and resolves them.
HowItWorksWorkflowStep12Description: Client approves the PR and pays the bounty via Maya credit card checkout.
HowItWorksWorkflowStep12Heading: Approve PR and Pay via Maya
HowItWorksWorkflowStep13Description: View all payment transactions and their status.
HowItWorksWorkflowStep14Description: Configure payout method and verify withdrawal balance.
HowItWorksWorkflowStep1Description: Authenticate with Google, GitHub, Microsoft, or Okta.
HowItWorksWorkflowStep2Description: Complete identity verification to enable payments and comply with regulations.
HowItWorksWorkflowStep3Description: Post a task with a bounty for developers to complete.
HowItWorksWorkflowStep4Description: Upload the source code folder to the task so developers can see the diff.
HowItWorksWorkflowStep5Description: Developers browse available tasks and find work that matches their skills.
HowItWorksWorkflowStep6Description: View the task description, bounty amount, and existing pull requests.
HowItWorksWorkflowStep7Description: Edit the source code directly in the browser diff editor.
HowItWorksWorkflowStep8Description: Submit your code changes as a pull request for the task.
HowItWorksWorkflowStep9Description: Client reviews the diff to see all code changes submitted by the developer.
LicensesCodeOwnershipDescription: Developers retain full copyright ownership of all code they submit through PrTask. Upon approval and payment of a bounty, the developer grants the client a perpetual, worldwide, non-exclusive, irrevocable license to use, modify, reproduce, and distribute the submitted code, subject to the open source license of the repository. PrTask does not claim ownership of any code submitted by developers. Developers must warrant that they have the right to submit all code and that it does not infringe third-party intellectual property rights. AI-generated code must be disclosed in the pull request description, and the developer must warrant they have the right to license it.
LicensesContactDescription: For licensing inquiries, compliance questions, or credit card processing concerns, contact compliance at prtask.com.
LicensesCreditCardDescription: Credit card payments on PrTask are processed by licensed, PCI DSS-compliant payment providers. PrTask does not store, process, or transmit credit card numbers, CVVs, or other sensitive cardholder data on its own servers. All credit card transactions are handled entirely by our payment processor.
LicensesDataProtectionDescription: PrTask uses HTTPS/TLS encryption for all communications. Authentication is handled via OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect (OIDC) with PKCE. No plaintext credentials are stored. See our Privacy Policy for full data handling details.
LicensesDescriptionParagraph1: This Licenses page provides comprehensive information about the software licenses, payment provider agreements, regulatory compliance certifications, and intellectual property policies that govern the PrTask platform. It covers the platform license itself, credit card processing compliance, payment provider integrations, data protection certifications, and the open source components that PrTask is built upon. Understanding these licenses helps you know your rights and obligations when using the platform.
LicensesDescriptionParagraph2: PrTask is built using open source technologies including .NET, Blazor, and various community libraries, and we are committed to respecting and acknowledging the licenses of all components we use. Code submitted by developers through pull requests remains the intellectual property of the submitter unless the task specifies otherwise, and the license terms for contributed code are determined by the repository license of the client's project. This page also details third-party service integrations with GitHub, payment processors, and Cloudflare that are essential to platform operations.
LicensesDeveloperCodeDescription: Pull requests submitted by developers remain the intellectual property of their authors until a bounty is paid. Upon payment, the submitted code is licensed to the client under the license specified in the task's repository. PrTask does not claim ownership of any code submitted by developers.
LicensesOpenSourceDescription: PrTask is built with .NET, Blazor WebAssembly, and other open source technologies. These components are used under their respective licenses (MIT, Apache 2.0, etc.). Full attribution is available in the project dependency files.
LicensesOpenSourceGratitude: We are grateful to the open source community for making these tools available. For a complete list of dependencies and their licenses, see the project dependency manifest.
LicensesPaymentProviderDescription: Our payment providers maintain current PCI DSS Level 1 certification, the highest level of security compliance in the payment card industry. They are licensed to process Visa, Mastercard, and other major card networks as applicable.
LicensesPlatformDescription: PrTask is a proprietary platform operated by PrTask. All rights to the platform, its design, branding, and source code are reserved. Use of the platform is subject to the Terms of Service.
LicensesRegulatoryDescription: PrTask complies with applicable laws and regulations regarding online payments, data protection, and consumer rights. For questions about our compliance posture, contact compliance at prtask.com.
LicensesThirdPartyCloudflare: Cloudflare: used for hosting, CDN, and DDoS protection, subject to Cloudflare's Terms of Service
LicensesThirdPartyDescription: PrTask incorporates the following third-party libraries and services. We gratefully acknowledge the contributions of these open-source projects and their maintainers: Microsoft .NET Runtime (MIT License), ASP.NET Core and Blazor (MIT License), Iced x86 Disassembler (MIT License), and various NuGet packages listed in our project files. For a complete list of dependencies and their licenses, see the project dependency manifest.
LicensesThirdPartyGitHub: GitHub: used for OAuth authentication and pull request integration, subject to GitHub's Terms of Service
LicensesThirdPartyPayment: Payment processors (including Maya Business): used for secure transaction handling, subject to their respective service agreements
LicensesUserContentDescription: By posting content on PrTask (including task descriptions, comments, and reviews), you grant PrTask a non-exclusive, worldwide, royalty-free license to display, reproduce, and distribute that content solely for the purpose of operating and improving the platform. You retain all ownership rights to your content. This license terminates when you delete your content from PrTask, except where the content has been shared with or relied upon by other users.
LoginBrowseTasksDescription: Explore available tasks filtered by language, framework, or bounty amount.
LoginDescriptionHeading: Sign In to PrTask — Secure Authentication Options
LoginDescriptionParagraph1: PrTask uses single sign-on (SSO) authentication through trusted identity providers to ensure your account security. You can sign in using your existing GitHub, Google, Microsoft Azure, or Okta account — no need to create a separate username and password for PrTask. This approach leverages the enterprise-grade security of these providers, including their two-factor authentication, suspicious login detection, and account recovery mechanisms, so your PrTask account benefits from the same protections you already have in place.
LoginDescriptionParagraph2: Authentication is required to access most features on PrTask because financial transactions and code contributions require verified identities. When you sign in for the first time, PrTask creates your account automatically and links it to your chosen identity provider. Developers can connect their GitHub accounts to streamline the pull request submission process, while clients benefit from verified identities that build trust in the marketplace. All authentication data is transmitted over encrypted connections and PrTask never stores your identity provider passwords.
LoginDescriptionParagraph3: For developers, signing in unlocks the ability to browse tasks, submit pull requests, earn bounties, and track your leaderboard ranking. For clients, authentication enables task creation, bounty funding, pull request review, and payment management. PrTask supports multiple connected accounts, so you can link your GitHub for code submissions and your Google account for general authentication. Visit the Settings page after signing in to manage your connected accounts, set up payment methods, and complete your KYC verification for bounty withdrawals.
LoginEarnMoneyDescription: Get paid for every merged pull request. Bounties are funded upfront and released on merge.
LoginGetPaidDescription: Once your PR is reviewed and merged, the bounty is released to your account automatically.
LoginHelpDescription: Visit our documentation for guides, FAQs, and support resources.
LoginNoPlatformFeesDescription: Developers keep 100% of the bounty. PrTask charges clients, never contributors.
LoginPublicPrsDescription: All work ships as real pull requests on GitHub. Your contributions are visible and verifiable.
LoginSignInDescription: Authenticate with your preferred SSO provider to create your PrTask account.
LoginSubmitPrsDescription: Claim a task, write the code, and open a pull request against the target repository.
PricingDescriptionParagraph1: PrTask operates on a fundamentally developer-friendly pricing model: developers pay nothing and keep 100% of every bounty they earn. There are no subscription fees, no commission percentages, and no hidden charges deducted from developer payouts. When a client posts a task with a $500 bounty, the developer who completes and gets their pull request approved receives the full $500. This pricing structure reflects our belief that the people writing the code should receive the full value of their work.
PricingDescriptionParagraph2: For clients, PrTask is also free to use. Clients pay only the bounty amount they set when creating tasks, plus standard payment processing fees charged by the payment gateway (credit card, bank transfer, or mobile wallet). There are no platform subscription fees, no markup on bounties, and no ongoing costs. Enterprise clients who need custom integrations, dedicated support, or volume task management can contact us for tailored solutions. Our pricing transparency extends to the feature comparison table above, which confirms that all core features are included for both developers and clients at no cost.
PricingDescriptionParagraph3: Payment processing on PrTask supports multiple methods to accommodate users worldwide. Clients can fund bounties using credit cards, bank transfers, GCash, GrabPay, Maya, and QR Ph payments. Developer payouts are processed automatically when a pull request is approved, with disbursement available via bank transfer or PayPal. The minimum bounty amount ensures meaningful compensation for development work, and all transactions are processed securely through PCI DSS compliant payment infrastructure. Review our refund policy for details on how payments are handled in case of disputes or task cancellations.
PrivacyContactDescription: For privacy inquiries, data subject requests, or to exercise any of your rights under GDPR, CCPA, RA 10173, LGPD, or any other applicable data protection law, contact our Data Protection Officer at dpo@prtask.com. For general privacy inquiries, contact privacy@prtask.com. PrTask will respond to all data subject requests within the timeframe required by applicable law (30 days for GDPR, 45 days for CCPA, 15 days for LGPD, 30 days for PIPEDA/Singapore PDPA). You also have the right to lodge a complaint with the supervisory authority in your jurisdiction.
PrivacyDataRetentionDescription: Account data is retained while your account is active. After account deletion, we retain: transaction records for 5 years as required by applicable tax regulations and AML record-keeping requirements; breach records for 24 months as required by PIPEDA; KYC verification records for 5 years as required by anti-money laundering laws. Anonymized and aggregated usage statistics may be retained indefinitely. We will delete or anonymize your personal data when it is no longer needed for the purpose for which it was collected, unless retention is required by law. You may request deletion of your data at any time, subject to these legal retention requirements.
PrivacyDescriptionParagraph1: This Privacy Policy explains in detail how PrTask collects, uses, stores, and protects your personal information across all our services and platforms. It covers data from account registration, task interactions, pull request submissions, payment processing, and general platform usage. PrTask is committed to data minimization — we collect only the information necessary to operate the marketplace, process payments, and comply with legal requirements.
PrivacyDescriptionParagraph2: This policy addresses compliance with privacy laws from over 20 jurisdictions worldwide, including the GDPR (European Union), UK GDPR, CCPA (California), RA 10173 (Philippines), LGPD (Brazil), PIPEDA (Canada), APPI (Japan), PDPA (Singapore and Thailand), POPIA (South Africa), and more. Each section outlines your specific rights under the applicable law and how to exercise them. PrTask never sells your personal data and provides mechanisms for data access, correction, deletion, and portability upon request.
PrivacyIntroduction: PrTask is committed to protecting your privacy and personal data. This Privacy Policy explains how PrTask collects, uses, shares, and protects your personal information when you use our platform. This policy applies to all users worldwide and addresses the specific requirements of data protection laws in the European Union, United Kingdom, California (USA), Philippines, Canada, Brazil, Japan, Singapore, Thailand, South Africa, Australia, India, South Korea, China, and New Zealand.
RefundAbandonedDescription: If a task receives no pull request submissions, the client may close it at any time. Since no payment is made until PR approval, there is nothing to refund for abandoned tasks.
RefundAfterApprovalDescription: Once a client approves a pull request and is charged, the bounty is paid to the developer. Refunds are only issued in cases of platform error, fraudulent activity, or if the submitted work is materially different from what was described in the PR.
RefundAutoPaymentDescription: PrTask uses an automatic payment model. Clients are only charged when they approve a pull request. No upfront payment or deposit is required when posting a task.
RefundChargebackDescription: If a client initiates a chargeback through their bank or credit card issuer instead of using our dispute resolution process, PrTask reserves the right to suspend the client's account pending investigation. Clients who file chargebacks without first attempting resolution through PrTask may have their accounts permanently terminated. We encourage all clients to use our built-in dispute and refund process before contacting their financial institution, as chargebacks incur fees and delays for all parties involved. PrTask reserves the right to pursue recovery of funds through appropriate legal channels.
RefundContactDescription: For refund requests or questions about this policy, contact support@prtask.com with your task ID and transaction reference number. For EU consumers exercising the right of withdrawal, include "EU Withdrawal" in your subject line. For Philippine consumer complaints, you may also contact the DTI directly.
RefundDescriptionParagraph1: This Refund Policy outlines the conditions under which payments made through PrTask may be refunded. Because PrTask uses an automatic payment model where bounties are disbursed upon pull request approval, the refund process is designed to be fair to both clients and developers. The policy covers scenarios including disputed work quality, abandoned tasks, platform errors, and regulatory requirements under EU and Philippine consumer protection laws.
RefundDescriptionParagraph2: PrTask does not charge developers until a pull request is approved, and clients are not charged until they explicitly fund a task bounty. This pay-on-approval model minimizes the need for refunds by ensuring that payment only occurs when both parties agree on the delivered work. If a refund is necessary, PrTask processes it through the original payment method within the timelines specified in this policy. Contact support@prtask.com to initiate a refund request or dispute a transaction.
RefundDisputeDescription: If a client believes an approved PR does not meet the task requirements, PrTask initiates an arbitration process. Both parties must submit evidence within 7 business days. Possible outcomes include full refund to client if work does not meet requirements, full payment to developer if work meets the stated task requirements, or partial refund if work partially meets requirements (split determined by PrTask).
RefundEuWithdrawalDescription: If you are an EU or EEA resident, you have a 14-day right of withdrawal for digital services under the Consumer Rights Directive (2011/83/EU). This right begins from the date of the transaction. You may exercise this right without giving any reason by contacting support@prtask.com within 14 days. PrTask will process your refund within 14 days of receiving your withdrawal notification using the same payment method as the original transaction. This withdrawal right may be waived at the point of purchase if you give explicit prior consent and acknowledge that you lose the right of withdrawal once the digital service (bounty payment to developer) is fully performed. If PrTask fails to inform you of the withdrawal right, it is automatically extended to 1 year.
RefundHowToDescription: To request a refund, log in to your PrTask account, navigate to the task in question, and select "Request Refund." Alternatively, email support at prtask.com with your task ID and reason for the refund request. All refund requests are reviewed within 2 business days.
RefundIntroduction: This Refund Policy describes when and how refunds are issued on the PrTask platform. PrTask uses an automatic payment model where clients are charged only upon approval of a pull request. This policy covers all refund scenarios, including applicable consumer protection rights under EU, Philippine, and other jurisdictions' laws.
RefundMethodDescription: Refunds are processed to the original payment method used. Processing times depend on your bank or card issuer but typically take 5-7 business days after approval.
RefundNoChargeDescription: Clients are not charged until they explicitly approve a pull request. If no PR is approved, no payment is made. Clients may cancel a task at any time before approving a PR at no cost.
RefundNoEscrowDescription: PrTask does not hold funds in escrow. Bounties are charged directly to the client at the moment of PR approval and paid to the developer. There is no intermediary holding period. This ensures developers receive payment promptly and clients are only charged for approved work.
RefundNonRefundableDescription: The following are not eligible for refunds: bounties for pull requests that were reviewed, approved, and where the code has been merged and meets the task requirements; disputes filed more than 30 days after the transaction; and situations where the client has already used, deployed, or incorporated the submitted code into production. EU residents' statutory withdrawal rights are not affected by this section where those rights have not been validly waived.
RefundPhConsumerDescription: Philippine residents are entitled to consumer protection under Republic Act No. 7394 (Consumer Act of the Philippines). If a service fails to meet the description or quality standards represented at the time of purchase, you are entitled to a full refund or remediation. You may file a complaint with the Department of Trade and Industry (DTI) if your refund request is not resolved satisfactorily through PrTask's internal process. PrTask will cooperate fully with any DTI investigation.
RefundPlatformErrorDescription: If a payment is processed incorrectly due to a platform error, PrTask will issue a full refund within 3 business days. Contact support at prtask.com with your transaction reference number.
RefundTimelineDescription: PrTask processes refunds within the following timelines: EU withdrawal right refunds within 14 days of receiving the withdrawal notice; platform error refunds within 3 business days; dispute resolution refunds within 5 business days of the resolution decision; and all other eligible refunds within 5 business days of approval. Actual receipt of the refund depends on your bank or payment provider and may take an additional 5-10 business days.
RepositoryDescriptionHeading: Repository Management — Connect and Configure Your GitHub Repos
RepositoryDescriptionParagraph1: The Repository page allows clients to connect their GitHub repositories to PrTask for seamless task management and pull request workflows. By linking a repository, you enable developers to submit pull requests directly against your codebase when working on tasks you have posted. Each connected repository can have a custom display name for easy identification within PrTask, and visibility settings control whether the repository is publicly listed or restricted to specific developers.
RepositoryDescriptionParagraph2: Managing your connected repositories is essential for maintaining an organized development workflow on PrTask. When you create a task, you link it to one of your connected repositories so developers know exactly where their code contributions should be submitted. Public repositories are visible to all marketplace participants, making them ideal for open source projects and community-driven development. Private repositories restrict access to invited developers only, suitable for proprietary codebases where you want to control who can view and contribute to your code.
RepositoryDescriptionParagraph3: PrTask integrates with GitHub through standard API connections, requiring only the repository URL to establish the link. You maintain full control over your repositories — PrTask does not modify repository settings, branch protections, or access permissions. All pull request reviews and merges are performed through your existing GitHub workflow, with PrTask handling the bounty payment and task status tracking automatically. As you add more repositories, the Repository page serves as your central directory for all codebases connected to the PrTask marketplace.
RepositoryEmptyState: No repositories added yet. Add a repository to start posting tasks.
RepositoryPageDescription: Manage your repositories and control code visibility for tasks.
RepositoryVisibilityPrivate: Private - Only assigned developers can view the code
RepositoryVisibilityPublic: Public - Anyone can view the source code
SettingsDescriptionHeading: Account Settings — Configure Your PrTask Experience
SettingsDescriptionParagraph1: The Settings page is your central hub for configuring every aspect of your PrTask account. From here, you can update your profile information including display name, avatar, and email address. You can configure notification preferences to control which events trigger email alerts — choose to receive all notifications, only important ones like payment confirmations and review requests, or disable email notifications entirely. Your profile settings determine how other users see you on the platform, including on the leaderboard and in pull request reviews.
SettingsDescriptionParagraph2: Payment configuration is a critical part of your account settings. Developers need to set up a withdrawal method (bank transfer or PayPal) to receive bounty payouts, while clients configure deposit methods (credit card or bank transfer) to fund task bounties. PrTask supports multiple payment gateways including QR Ph, credit cards, GCash, GrabPay, Maya, and direct bank transfers — each can be individually enabled or disabled based on your preference. You can also manage saved payment cards, configure automatic invoicing, and set up QR code-based payments for quick transactions.
SettingsDescriptionParagraph3: Security and verification settings protect your account and enable access to all platform features. Connect multiple identity providers (Google, GitHub, Microsoft, Okta) to your account for flexible authentication options. Enable two-factor authentication via authenticator app or email for additional security. Complete the KYC (Know Your Customer) verification process by submitting a government-issued ID to unlock higher withdrawal limits and build trust with clients. Privacy settings let you control the visibility of your profile and activity, and timezone configuration ensures dates and deadlines display correctly for your location.
SettingsGatewaysDescription: Enable or disable payment methods for your account.
SettingsNoSavedCards: No saved cards. Add a card below.
SettingsPrivacyProfilePrivate: Private - Only you can view your profile
SettingsPrivacyProfilePublic: Public - Anyone can view your profile
SettingsSavedCardsDescription: Manage your saved credit and debit cards for quick payments.
SprintDescriptionHeading: Sprint Management — Organize Time-Boxed Development Work
SprintDescriptionParagraph1: Sprints on PrTask bring agile project management methodology to the bounty-based development model. A sprint is a time-boxed period — typically one to four weeks — during which specific tasks are planned, executed, and delivered. Sprint management allows clients and team leads to organize related tasks into focused work periods with clear start dates, end dates, and goals. This structured approach helps teams maintain momentum, track progress against deadlines, and ensure that development work aligns with broader project milestones.
SprintDescriptionParagraph2: Creating a sprint is straightforward: give it a name, set a goal that describes what should be accomplished, and define the start and end dates. Sprints progress through three statuses — Planned, Active, and Completed — giving you clear visibility into where each sprint stands. You can start planned sprints when the team is ready, complete active sprints when the time box expires, or delete sprints that are no longer needed. Each sprint tracks the tasks assigned to it, providing a focused view of what needs to be done within the time period.
SprintDescriptionParagraph3: Sprint management on PrTask integrates with the broader task and board systems to provide a complete project management experience. Assign tasks from the task list to specific sprints to create a sprint backlog, track completion rates as developers submit pull requests, and review sprint outcomes to improve planning for future iterations. Whether you are running a small open source project with a handful of contributors or managing a large-scale development effort across multiple repositories, sprint management helps you maintain predictable delivery cadences and keep your development work organized and on schedule.
SprintSeoDescription: Organize your open source contributions into sprints. Track pull request progress, earn bounties, and manage your developer workflow. A great side hustle for developers who want to earn money through quality code.
TermsAcceptedPaymentDescription: Clients pay bounties via supported payment methods including credit cards. Developers receive payouts through their chosen method. Developers must configure their preferred payout method in their account settings before submitting pull requests.
TermsAccountCreationDescription: To use PrTask, you must create an account by authenticating through a supported single sign-on (SSO) provider such as GitHub, Google, Microsoft, or Okta. You are responsible for maintaining the security of your account credentials and for all activities that occur under your account. You must provide accurate and complete information during registration and keep your account information up to date. You may not create more than one account or share your account with others.
TermsAccountTerminationDescription: PrTask reserves the right to suspend or terminate accounts that violate these Terms, engage in fraudulent activity, or abuse the platform. Upon termination, your right to use the platform ceases immediately. Any pending bounties may be forfeited. PrTask will retain transaction records as required by applicable tax and AML regulations. You may request deletion of your personal data subject to legal retention requirements by contacting privacy@prtask.com. EU users retain the right to cancel their contract as provided by the Consumer Rights Directive.
TermsAmlComplianceDescription: PrTask complies with Republic Act No. 9160 (Anti-Money Laundering Act) as amended by RA 11521, EU Anti-Money Laundering Directives (AMLD6), and applicable AML laws in all jurisdictions. All users must complete Know Your Customer (KYC) verification before receiving payouts. PrTask monitors transactions for suspicious activity and reports covered and suspicious transactions to the Anti-Money Laundering Council (AMLC) and relevant authorities as required by law. Users must not use PrTask to launder money, finance terrorism, or facilitate any illegal financial activity. PrTask reserves the right to freeze accounts and withhold funds pending investigation of suspicious activity.
TermsArbitrationDescription: All disputes arising out of or relating to these Terms or your use of PrTask that are not resolved through the internal dispute resolution process shall be resolved through binding arbitration administered by a mutually agreed arbitral institution. You agree to waive the right to a jury trial and to participate in a class action. You may opt out of this arbitration agreement within 30 days of account creation by sending written notice to legal@prtask.com. Small claims court actions are exempt from this arbitration requirement. EU residents retain the right to access Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR) mechanisms and may file complaints with the applicable consumer arbitration board in their Member State.
TermsAutoPaymentDescription: Clients are automatically charged the bounty amount when they approve a pull request. By approving a PR, the client authorizes the charge. No upfront deposit is required.
TermsClientObligationsDescription: As a client on PrTask, you agree to: provide clear and accurate task descriptions and requirements; set fair and appropriate bounty amounts for the work requested; review submitted pull requests in a timely and good-faith manner; authorize payment upon approving a pull request; maintain a valid payment method on your account; not post fraudulent, misleading, or illegal tasks; and comply with all applicable laws and regulations in your jurisdiction.
TermsConfidentialityDescription: Users agree to keep confidential any non-public information obtained through the platform, including but not limited to private repository code, client business requirements, and payment details of other users.
TermsConsumerProtectionDescription: PrTask complies with Republic Act No. 7394 (Consumer Act of the Philippines) and equivalent consumer protection laws in all jurisdictions where the platform operates. Users are entitled to fair and equitable treatment, accurate information about services and pricing, and access to effective complaint and redress mechanisms. PrTask maintains transparent pricing with no hidden fees, provides clear service descriptions for all tasks and bounties, and ensures that users can exercise their consumer rights without obstruction. EU users benefit from the protections of the Consumer Rights Directive (2011/83/EU), including pre-contractual information requirements and the right to cancel distance contracts.
TermsContactDescription: For questions about these Terms of Service, contact us at legal@prtask.com. For privacy inquiries, contact our Data Protection Officer at dpo@prtask.com. For accessibility concerns, contact accessibility@prtask.com. For abuse reports, contact abuse@prtask.com.
TermsContentModerationDescription: In compliance with the EU Digital Services Act (DSA) 2022/2065, PrTask maintains transparent content moderation policies. Users may flag illegal or harmful content through our reporting mechanism. PrTask provides a statement of reasons for every content moderation decision and offers an internal complaint handling mechanism for users affected by moderation decisions. Users have access to out-of-court dispute settlement. PrTask publishes annual transparency reports on content moderation activities. PrTask prohibits dark patterns in all user interfaces and ensures that content moderation decisions are made in a non-discriminatory manner with due regard for fundamental rights.
TermsDescriptionParagraph1: These Terms of Service constitute the complete legal agreement between you and PrTask governing the use of our platform. They cover all aspects of the developer marketplace including account creation, task posting, pull request submissions, payment processing, intellectual property rights, dispute resolution, and platform conduct requirements. The terms are designed to protect both developers and clients while maintaining a fair, transparent marketplace for bounty-based software development.
TermsDescriptionParagraph2: PrTask operates under Philippine law and complies with international regulations including GDPR for European users, CCPA for California residents, and consumer protection laws across multiple jurisdictions. These terms address platform fees (zero for developers, transparent for clients), automatic payment processing upon pull request approval, refund rights and dispute resolution procedures, content moderation policies, and KYC/AML compliance requirements. We encourage all users to read these terms thoroughly and contact legal@prtask.com with any questions.
TermsDeveloperObligationsDescription: As a developer on PrTask, you agree to: submit only original work that you have the right to license; disclose the use of AI-generated code in your pull request descriptions; comply with the open source license requirements of each task's repository; complete KYC identity verification before receiving any payouts; not submit plagiarized, stolen, or unlicensed code; maintain professional conduct in all platform interactions; and comply with all applicable laws and regulations in your jurisdiction, including tax reporting obligations.
TermsDisclaimerDescription: The PrTask platform is provided on an "as is" and "as available" basis without warranties of any kind, whether express or implied. PrTask disclaims all warranties, including implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, title, and non-infringement. PrTask does not guarantee the quality, accuracy, or completeness of any work submitted by developers, nor does it guarantee that the platform will be uninterrupted, secure, or error-free. This disclaimer does not affect mandatory consumer rights that cannot be excluded under applicable law, including EU consumer protection regulations.
TermsDisputeDescription: All disputes arising out of or relating to these Terms or your use of PrTask shall be resolved through the following process: (1) Users must first submit a written complaint to support@prtask.com describing the issue and desired resolution; (2) PrTask will acknowledge the complaint within 3 business days and investigate within 14 business days; (3) If the complaint is not resolved to the user's satisfaction, the dispute shall proceed to mediation administered by a mutually agreed mediator; (4) If mediation fails, the dispute shall be resolved through binding arbitration as described in the Arbitration section below. Users may also file complaints with the Department of Trade and Industry (DTI) or other applicable consumer protection agencies in their jurisdiction.
TermsEligibilityDescription: You must be at least 18 years old and legally capable of entering into binding contracts in your jurisdiction to use PrTask. By creating an account, you represent and warrant that you meet these eligibility requirements. PrTask is not intended for users under 18 years of age in any jurisdiction. Where applicable law sets a higher age of majority, you must meet that threshold.
TermsEntireAgreementDescription: These Terms, together with the Privacy Policy, Cookie Policy, Acceptable Use Policy, Refund Policy, DMCA Policy, and any other policies referenced herein, constitute the entire agreement between you and PrTask regarding your use of the platform. These Terms supersede all prior agreements, understandings, and communications between you and PrTask, whether oral or written.
TermsEuConsumerRightsDescription: EU residents have a 14-day right of withdrawal for digital services under the Consumer Rights Directive. This right may be waived at the point of purchase with your explicit consent and acknowledgment that you lose the withdrawal right once the digital service is fully performed. PrTask provides clear pre-contractual information about prices, main characteristics of services, and trader identity. From June 2026, PrTask will provide a clear "Cancel my contract" function as required by EU law. PrTask participates in Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR) as required by the Digital Services Act. Users may contact the applicable consumer arbitration board in their EU Member State for out-of-court dispute settlement.
TermsForceDescription: PrTask shall not be liable for any failure or delay in performing its obligations where such failure or delay results from events beyond its reasonable control, including but not limited to natural disasters, war, terrorism, epidemics, internet outages, or government actions.
TermsGoverningLawDescription: These Terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Republic of the Philippines, without regard to conflict of law principles. Any legal action not subject to arbitration shall be brought exclusively in the courts of the Republic of the Philippines. Notwithstanding the foregoing, mandatory consumer protection laws of your jurisdiction of residence may apply to the extent they cannot be excluded by contract. EU residents retain all rights under applicable EU consumer protection regulations regardless of governing law selection.
TermsIndemnificationDescription: You agree to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless PrTask, its officers, directors, employees, and agents from and against any claims, liabilities, damages, losses, and expenses (including reasonable legal fees) arising out of or related to your use of the platform, your violation of these Terms, or your infringement of any third-party rights. This indemnification obligation does not apply to the extent prohibited by applicable mandatory consumer protection laws in your jurisdiction.
TermsIntellectualPropertyDescription: All code submitted through PrTask remains the intellectual property of its author until a bounty is paid. Upon payment, the submitted code is licensed to the client under the repository's license. PrTask retains no ownership of user-submitted code.
TermsIntroduction: Welcome to PrTask. These Terms of Service govern your access to and use of the PrTask platform, a developer marketplace where clients post tasks with bounties and developers submit pull requests for payment. By creating an account or using PrTask, you agree to be bound by these Terms. If you do not agree, do not use the platform.
TermsLiabilityDescription: To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, PrTask and its officers, employees, and affiliates shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, including loss of profits, data, or goodwill, arising from your use of the platform. PrTask's total aggregate liability for any claims shall not exceed the total fees paid by or to you through the platform in the twelve months preceding the claim. This limitation does not apply to liability that cannot be excluded or limited under applicable law, including mandatory consumer protection laws in the EU, Philippines, and other jurisdictions.
TermsLicenseGrantsDescription: Upon approval and payment of a bounty, the developer grants the client a perpetual, worldwide, non-exclusive, irrevocable license to use, modify, reproduce, and distribute the submitted code, subject to any open source license requirements of the repository. The developer retains copyright ownership of their code. PrTask is granted a non-exclusive, worldwide license to display, cache, and reproduce submitted code solely for the purpose of operating and improving the platform. Developers represent and warrant that they have the legal right to grant these licenses and that their submissions are free of third-party intellectual property claims.
TermsModificationDescription: PrTask reserves the right to modify these Terms at any time. Material changes will be communicated via email or a prominent notice on the platform at least 30 days before taking effect. Your continued use of PrTask after the effective date constitutes acceptance of the updated Terms. If you do not agree to the changes, you must discontinue use and close your account. Previous versions of these Terms are available upon request.
TermsPaymentDescription: When a client approves a pull request, they are automatically charged the bounty amount via their registered payment method. The full bounty is then paid to the developer. All transactions are in US Dollars (USD). There are no platform fees.
TermsPlatformFeeDescription: PrTask is a free platform. No fees are charged to clients or developers. Developers receive 100% of the bounty amount upon PR approval.
TermsPlatformRoleDescription: PrTask acts as an intermediary connecting clients who post tasks with developers who submit pull requests. PrTask does not employ developers and is not responsible for the quality of work delivered.
TermsPrivacyDescription: Your use of PrTask is subject to our Privacy Policy. By using the platform, you consent to the collection and use of your information as described in the Privacy Policy.
TermsPublicPrDescription: All pull requests submitted on PrTask are public. Multiple developers may submit pull requests for the same task. The client reviews all submissions and chooses which pull request to approve. Only the approved PR results in payment.
TermsRefundRightsDescription: Clients may request a refund if a pull request was approved in error or if the delivered work materially fails to meet the task requirements. Refund requests must be submitted within 7 days of payment. PrTask will review refund requests within 5 business days and process eligible refunds through the original payment method. For EU residents, the 14-day right of withdrawal for digital services applies as detailed in our Refund Policy. For Philippine residents, consumer protection rights under RA 7394 are fully honored. See our full Refund Policy for complete details.
TermsSeverabilityDescription: If any provision of these terms is found to be unenforceable, the remaining provisions will continue in full force and effect. The unenforceable provision will be modified to the minimum extent necessary to make it enforceable.
TermsTaxDescription: Users are solely responsible for determining and fulfilling their own tax obligations arising from transactions on PrTask, including income tax reporting to the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) or equivalent authority in their jurisdiction. PrTask does not withhold taxes on behalf of users and does not provide tax advice. PrTask may collect tax identification information (W-9/W-8BEN for US persons, equivalent for other jurisdictions) and issue IRS Form 1099-K for US payees exceeding applicable thresholds. PrTask complies with DAC7 reporting for EU activities and Philippine VAT requirements under RA 12023. PrTask may generate transaction summaries to assist users with tax reporting, but users must independently verify their tax obligations.
TermsUserConductDescription: Users agree to use PrTask in a lawful manner. Prohibited activities include submitting plagiarized code, manipulating leaderboard rankings, creating multiple accounts, and engaging in fraudulent payment activities. See our Acceptable Use Policy for full details.
TermsWaiverDescription: The failure of PrTask to enforce any right or provision of these terms shall not constitute a waiver of such right or provision. Any waiver must be in writing and signed by an authorized representative of PrTask.
PricingFaqCurrencyAnswer: All transactions are in US Dollars (USD).
PricingFaqMethodsAnswer: We accept Visa, Mastercard, American Express, and other major credit and debit cards through our secure payment processor.
PricingFaqMinBountyAnswer: Yes, the minimum bounty that a client can set for a task is $1. There is no maximum limit.
PricingFaqMinBountyQuestion: Is there a minimum bounty amount?
PricingFaqPayoutTimeAnswer: Payouts are typically processed within 3 to 5 business days after the client approves your pull request.
PricingFaqPayoutTimeQuestion: How long until I receive my payout?
PricingFaqProcessAnswer: When a client approves a pull request, they are automatically charged the bounty amount via their payment method. The developer receives 100% of the bounty.
PricingFaqWhenPaidAnswer: Payment is processed automatically when your PR is approved by the client. Developers receive 100% of the bounty via their preferred payout method.
PricingRefundDescription: We offer fair refund terms for disputed or undelivered work. Review our full refund policy for complete details.
PrivacyAppiDescription: If you are located in Japan, your personal information is protected under the Act on the Protection of Personal Information (APPI), enforced by the Personal Information Protection Commission (PPC). PrTask publishes the purpose of data use, implements cybersecurity and physical safeguards, and provides an opt-out mechanism for third-party data sharing. We will notify the PPC and affected individuals in the event of a data breach. You have the right to request disclosure, correction, cessation of use, and deletion of your personal information. Contact dpo@prtask.com to exercise your rights.
PrivacyAustraliaDescription: If you are located in Australia, your personal information is protected under the Privacy Act 1988 and the Australian Privacy Principles (APPs), enforced by the Office of the Australian Information Commissioner (OAIC). PrTask maintains an up-to-date privacy policy and participates in the Notifiable Data Breaches scheme. You have the right to access and correct your personal information. PrTask does not use automated decision-making that significantly affects you without disclosure. PrTask complies with AI disclosure requirements effective December 2026. Contact privacy@prtask.com to exercise your rights or file a complaint with the OAIC.
PrivacyAutomatedDecisionDescription: PrTask does not currently use automated decision-making or profiling that produces legal effects or similarly significant effects on users. If PrTask introduces such processing in the future, we will provide meaningful information about the logic involved, the significance, and the envisaged consequences, and will ensure that you have the right to obtain human intervention, express your point of view, and contest the decision, as required by GDPR Article 22 and equivalent provisions in other jurisdictions.
PrivacyCcpaCategoriesDescription: Under the California Consumer Privacy Act, the categories of personal information we have collected in the past 12 months include: identifiers (name, email address, username, IP address); commercial information (transaction history, bounty payments); internet or electronic network activity (browsing history on PrTask, feature usage, log data); professional information (GitHub profile, portfolio, skills); and geolocation data (approximate location derived from IP address). We do not collect sensitive personal information as defined by the CCPA. We do not sell or share your personal information with third parties for cross-context behavioral advertising purposes.
PrivacyCcpaContactInfo: To submit a CCPA/CPRA request, email privacy@prtask.com with the subject line "CCPA Request." We will verify your identity before processing the request and respond within 45 days. PrTask honors Global Privacy Control (GPC) signals as a valid opt-out of the sale or sharing of personal information.
PrivacyCcpaDescription: If you are a California resident, the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA), as amended by the California Privacy Rights Act (CPRA), grants you additional rights regarding your personal information. PrTask does not sell or share your personal information with third parties for cross-context behavioral advertising. PrTask provides the mandatory eleven disclosures required by the CCPA in this policy, including a 12-month lookback on data categories collected. Under the CCPA/CPRA, you have the right to:
PrivacyCcpaRightDeletion: Request deletion of your personal information, subject to legal exceptions
PrivacyCcpaRightKnow: Know what personal information we collect, use, and disclose about you, including a 12-month lookback
PrivacyCcpaRightLimit: Limit the use of sensitive personal information (PrTask does not collect sensitive personal information as defined by CCPA)
PrivacyCcpaRightNonDiscrimination: Non-discrimination for exercising your CCPA/CPRA rights
PrivacyCcpaRightOptOut: Opt out of the sale or sharing of your personal information (PrTask does not sell personal information)
PrivacyChangesDescription: PrTask may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Material changes will be communicated via email or a prominent notice on the platform at least 30 days before taking effect. We review and update this policy at least annually. The "Last updated" date at the top of this policy indicates the most recent revision. Your continued use of PrTask after the effective date of changes constitutes acceptance of the updated policy. Previous versions of this policy are available upon request.
PrivacyChildrensDescription: PrTask is not intended for users under 18 years of age. We do not knowingly collect personal data from minors. If you believe a minor has created an account, contact us for removal.
PrivacyControllerDescription: PrTask is the data controller responsible for your personal data. Our Data Protection Officer (DPO) can be reached at dpo@prtask.com. For general privacy inquiries, contact privacy@prtask.com. Our registered address is available on our Contact page. If you are located in the EU and PrTask does not have an EU establishment, we will appoint an EU representative as required by Article 27 of the GDPR.
PrivacyCookieReferenceDescription: PrTask uses cookies and similar technologies as described in our Cookie Policy. Essential cookies are required for platform operation. Analytics and marketing cookies require your opt-in consent in the EU, UK, and other jurisdictions that mandate prior consent. You can manage your cookie preferences at any time. See our Cookie Policy for full details including a complete inventory of cookies used, their purposes, and retention periods.
PrivacyDataBreachDescription: PrTask maintains a comprehensive data breach response plan. In the event of a personal data breach: (a) GDPR: PrTask will notify the relevant supervisory authority within 72 hours and affected individuals when the breach is likely to result in a high risk to their rights and freedoms; (b) Philippines RA 10173: PrTask will notify the National Privacy Commission (NPC) and affected individuals within 72 hours; (c) New Zealand: notification to the Privacy Commissioner and affected individuals within 72 hours for breaches likely to cause serious harm; (d) CCPA: notification to affected California residents within the timeframe required by law; (e) All jurisdictions: PrTask will document the breach, conduct a forensic investigation, implement containment measures, notify affected users with a description of the breach, the types of data involved, and recommended protective measures, and conduct a post-incident review. Our Data Protection Officer serves as the primary breach response coordinator. Contact dpo@prtask.com for data breach concerns.
PrivacyDataRightsDescription: You have the right to access your personal data held by PrTask, correct inaccurate or incomplete data, request deletion of your data (subject to legal retention requirements), and object to processing of your data.
PrivacyDataSecurityDescription: We use HTTPS encryption for all communications, GitHub OAuth tokens with minimal required scopes, and industry-standard security practices. However, no system is perfectly secure. Report vulnerabilities to security at prtask.com.
PrivacyDoNotSellDescription: PrTask does not sell your personal information to third parties. PrTask does not share your personal information with third parties for cross-context behavioral advertising. If this practice changes in the future, PrTask will provide a prominent "Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information" link and a "Limit the Use of My Sensitive Personal Information" link as required by the CCPA/CPRA. PrTask honors Global Privacy Control (GPC) signals as a valid opt-out request.
PrivacyDoNotSellHeading: Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information
PrivacyDpdpDescription: If you are located in India, your digital personal data is protected under the Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023 (DPDP), enforced by the Data Protection Board of India. PrTask obtains your consent through clear affirmative action before processing your data. You have the right to: access a summary of your personal data and processing activities; correct and update your data; erase your data; nominate another person to exercise your rights; and file a grievance. PrTask maintains a grievance redressal mechanism and will respond to your requests promptly. Contact dpo@prtask.com to exercise your rights or file a grievance.
PrivacyGdprContactInfo: To exercise any of these rights, contact our Data Protection Officer at dpo@prtask.com. We will respond within 30 days as required by the GDPR. PrTask recognizes and responds to the Global Privacy Control (GPC) signal as a valid opt-out request.
PrivacyGdprDescription: If you are located in the European Economic Area (EEA), your personal data is protected under the General Data Protection Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (GDPR). PrTask processes your data under the lawful bases of contract performance, legitimate interest, consent, and legal obligation as detailed in Section 4 above. PrTask implements privacy by design and by default (Article 25), maintains Records of Processing Activities (Article 30), and conducts Data Protection Impact Assessments for high-risk processing. PrTask recognizes and responds to Global Privacy Control (GPC) signals. As an EU data subject, you have the right to:
PrivacyGdprRightAccess: Access a copy of all personal data we hold about you
PrivacyGdprRightErase: Erase your personal data (right to be forgotten), subject to legal retention obligations
PrivacyGdprRightLodgeComplaint: Lodge a complaint with your local supervisory authority
PrivacyGdprRightObject: Object to processing based on legitimate interests, including profiling
PrivacyGdprRightPortability: Data portability: receive your data in a structured, machine-readable format
PrivacyGdprRightRectify: Rectify inaccurate or incomplete personal data
PrivacyGdprRightRestrict: Restrict processing of your personal data in certain circumstances
PrivacyGdprRightWithdrawConsent: Withdraw consent at any time where processing is based on consent
PrivacyInfoCollectedDescription: PrTask collects the following categories of personal data: (a) Account information: when you sign in with a supported SSO provider (GitHub, Google, Microsoft, Okta), we collect your username, display name, email address, and avatar URL; (b) Payment data: when you initiate or receive a payment, our payment provider processes payment details directly; developers provide their preferred payout method details; (c) Platform activity: task and pull request activity, code reviews, comments, and leaderboard participation; (d) Technical data: IP address, browser type, operating system, device information, and approximate geolocation derived from IP address; (e) KYC verification data: government-issued identification and selfie verification processed by our identity verification provider; (f) Tax information: tax identification numbers (W-9/W-8BEN) collected for tax compliance purposes.
PrivacyInfoSharingDescription: We share your information with the following categories of recipients: (a) Payment processors (including Maya Business and Stripe) for payment processing and developer payouts; (b) Identity verification providers for KYC compliance; (c) GitHub for authentication and repository integration; (d) Cloudflare for hosting, CDN, and security services; (e) Tax authorities when required by law (IRS, BIR, EU tax authorities under DAC7). Your public profile (username, badges, completed tasks) is visible on the leaderboard. We do not sell your personal data to third parties. We do not share your data with third parties for cross-context behavioral advertising. We may disclose information if required by applicable law, a valid court order, or to protect the safety and rights of PrTask and its users.
PrivacyInfoUsageDescription: We use your information to operate the PrTask platform, process bounty payments, display public leaderboard profiles and achievement badges, communicate with you about task status and payments, and prevent fraud or abuse.
PrivacyInternationalDescription: PrTask servers are hosted via Cloudflare and your data may be processed in multiple jurisdictions. For transfers of personal data from the EEA, UK, or other jurisdictions with data transfer restrictions, PrTask implements appropriate safeguards including: Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) approved by the European Commission; UK International Data Transfer Agreement or Addendum where applicable; adequacy decisions where the destination country has been deemed to provide adequate protection; and supplementary measures where required by transfer impact assessments. PrTask does not transfer data to countries without adequate safeguards in place. You may request a copy of the safeguards used for international transfers by contacting dpo@prtask.com.
PrivacyLegalBasisDescription: We process your personal data under the following lawful bases: (a) Contract performance: processing necessary to fulfill our contract with you, including account management, payment processing, and platform operation; (b) Legitimate interest: processing necessary for our legitimate interests, including fraud prevention, platform security, and service improvement, where these interests are not overridden by your rights; (c) Consent: where you have given specific consent for processing, such as marketing communications and non-essential cookies; (d) Legal obligation: processing necessary to comply with applicable laws, including tax reporting, AML compliance, and data breach notification requirements. You may withdraw consent at any time without affecting the lawfulness of processing based on consent before withdrawal.
PrivacyLgpdDescription: If you are located in Brazil, your personal data is protected under the Lei Geral de Protecao de Dados (LGPD), enforced by the ANPD. PrTask processes your data under lawful bases including consent, contract performance, and legitimate interest. You have the right to: confirmation of data processing; access your data; correct incomplete or inaccurate data; anonymize, block, or delete unnecessary data; data portability; information about sharing with third parties; information about consent withdrawal; and revoke consent at any time. PrTask will respond to data subject requests within 15 days. Contact our DPO at dpo@prtask.com to exercise your LGPD rights.
PrivacyNzDescription: If you are located in New Zealand, your personal information is protected under the Privacy Act 2020, enforced by the Office of the Privacy Commissioner. PrTask complies with the 13 Information Privacy Principles. You have the right to access and correct your personal information. PrTask will notify the Privacy Commissioner and affected individuals within 72 hours of any breach that causes or is likely to cause serious harm. PrTask complies with cross-border disclosure restrictions. Contact privacy@prtask.com to exercise your rights or file a complaint with the Privacy Commissioner.
PrivacyPaymentDataDescription: All client payment card data is handled exclusively by our payment provider. PrTask never stores credit card numbers or CVVs on our servers. Developer payout details are stored securely and shared only with the chosen payout provider. Transaction records (amount, status, date) are retained for accounting purposes.
PrivacyPdpaSingaporeDescription: If you are located in Singapore, your personal data is protected under the Personal Data Protection Act 2012 (PDPA), enforced by the Personal Data Protection Commission (PDPC). PrTask has appointed a Data Protection Officer whose contact information is publicly available. You have the right to access and correct your personal data within 30 days. PrTask will notify the PDPC of any data breach that is likely to result in significant harm or is of a significant scale. We retain your personal data only as long as necessary for the purpose for which it was collected. Contact dpo@prtask.com to exercise your rights.
PrivacyPdpaThailandDescription: If you are located in Thailand, your personal data is protected under the Personal Data Protection Act B.E. 2562 (PDPA), enforced by the Personal Data Protection Committee (PDPC). PrTask obtains consent before collecting, using, or disclosing your personal data. You have the right to: access your data; obtain a copy in a readable format; object to processing; request erasure or destruction; request suspension of use; and withdraw consent. PrTask implements appropriate security safeguards and conducts regular audits. Contact dpo@prtask.com to exercise your rights.
PrivacyPipaDescription: If you are located in South Korea, your personal information is protected under the Personal Information Protection Act (PIPA), enforced by the Personal Information Protection Commission (PIPC). PrTask obtains truly voluntary consent (not bundled or coercive) for data processing. You have the right to: access your data; correct inaccurate data; request suspension of processing; request deletion; and data portability in a secure machine-readable format (effective March 2025). PrTask provides transparency on algorithmic processes and user profiling. If PrTask does not have a domestic establishment, we will appoint a domestic representative as required. Contact dpo@prtask.com to exercise your rights.
PrivacyPipedaDescription: If you are a Canadian resident, your personal information is protected under the Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act (PIPEDA), enforced by the Office of the Privacy Commissioner of Canada (OPC). PrTask adheres to the ten Fair Information Principles: accountability, identifying purposes, consent, limiting collection, limiting use and disclosure, accuracy, safeguards, openness, individual access, and challenging compliance. You have the right to access your personal information and challenge its accuracy. PrTask will respond to access requests within 30 days. Contact privacy@prtask.com to exercise your rights or file a complaint.
PrivacyPiplDescription: If you are located in China, your personal information is protected under the Personal Information Protection Law (PIPL), enforced by the Cyberspace Administration of China (CAC). PrTask does not bundle consent for multiple processing purposes and obtains separate consent for sensitive personal information. You have the right to: know about and decide on processing; restrict or refuse processing; access and copy your data; data portability; correct inaccurate data; request deletion; and request an explanation of processing rules. PrTask conducts Personal Information Protection Impact Assessments as required. Contact dpo@prtask.com to exercise your rights.
PrivacyPopiaDescription: If you are located in South Africa, your personal information is protected under the Protection of Personal Information Act (POPIA), enforced by the Information Regulator. PrTask complies with the eight conditions for lawful processing: accountability, processing limitation, purpose specification, further processing limitation, information quality, openness, security safeguards, and data subject participation. You have the right to access, correct, and delete your personal information. PrTask will notify the Information Regulator and affected individuals of any security compromise as soon as reasonably possible. Contact dpo@prtask.com to exercise your rights.
PrivacyRa10173Description: If you are a Philippine citizen or resident, your personal data is protected under Republic Act No. 10173 (Data Privacy Act of 2012), enforced by the National Privacy Commission (NPC). PrTask complies with the principles of transparency, legitimate purpose, and proportionality. You have the right to: be informed of the purpose and extent of processing; access your personal data; object to processing; erasure or blocking of personal data; rectify inaccurate data; file a complaint with the NPC; and claim damages for unauthorized processing. PrTask has appointed a Data Protection Officer and will register with the NPC as required for entities processing sensitive personal information. Contact our DPO at dpo@prtask.com to exercise your rights.
PrivacyThirdPartyDescription: PrTask integrates with third-party services including GitHub for authentication and repository integration, payment processors (including Maya Business) for transaction handling, Cloudflare for hosting and CDN, and identity verification providers for KYC compliance. Each third-party service operates under its own privacy policy. We only share the minimum data necessary for each integration to function. We maintain Data Processing Agreements (DPAs) with all third-party processors as required by GDPR and applicable data protection laws.
PrivacyUkGdprDescription: If you are located in the United Kingdom, your personal data is protected under the UK GDPR and the Data Protection Act 2018, enforced by the Information Commissioner's Office (ICO). You have the same rights as EU data subjects listed above, adapted to UK law. PrTask maintains a lawful basis for processing UK residents' data and implements appropriate technical and organizational security measures. If PrTask does not have a UK establishment, we will appoint a UK representative as required. To exercise your rights or file a complaint, contact our DPO at dpo@prtask.com or the ICO at ico.org.uk.
PrivacyUkGdprHeading: UK GDPR and Data Protection Act 2018
PaymentSeoDescription: View your payment history and transaction details.
PaymentDescriptionParagraph3: For clients, this page also serves as a record of all bounty disbursements made to developers who completed your tasks. You can cross-reference payments with specific tasks to verify that funds were distributed correctly. If you have questions about a specific transaction or need to dispute a payment, PrTask provides a dedicated support channel accessible from your account settings. All payment data is encrypted and stored securely in compliance with PCI DSS standards.
PaymentDescriptionParagraph2: PrTask processes payments automatically when a pull request is approved and merged by the task owner. Developers receive 100% of the bounty amount with no platform fees deducted from their earnings. Payment statuses update in real time, allowing you to see whether a transaction is pending, completed, or requires attention. You can filter your payment history by entering your user ID to view transactions specific to your account.
PaymentDescriptionParagraph1: The Payment History page provides a comprehensive view of all financial transactions associated with your PrTask account. Whether you are a developer receiving bounty payouts or a client funding task bounties, this page gives you full transparency into every payment made through the platform. Each transaction record includes the date, associated task, amount, current status, and payment type so you can track your finances with confidence.
